title: Visual QA Configuration
description: What the project DECLARES for Visual QA — the things an agent must not guess: the design-intent anchor (required) and the web routes to judge (required). v1 does not infer these.

$schema.description: Editor hint (the relative schema path) — allowed, not part of the config.

setup.description: OPTIONAL project-specific 'how to make my UI reachable' — authored by you (or your agent), the same trust as a project CLAUDE.md (NOT a security input). The skill follows this before capturing. Most web apps need nothing (the dev server is already up). A VS Code extension (a webview with no URL) sets command to serve its preview harness + readyUrl to wait on, then points routes at the harness URLs.

command.description: A shell command to start/serve the UI (e.g. 'npm run dev' or 'npm run preview:webview'). The agent runs it in the background and waits for readyUrl before capturing.

readyUrl.description: A URL that returns 200 once the UI is ready (the agent polls it after running command).

notes.description: Any other prelude an agent must do/know before capture (auth, seeding, which fixture states to render).

anchor.description: The design-intent anchor the judge compares against. Provide AT LEAST ONE of text/path/url. Without an anchor a verdict is just taste — the skill returns 'unable_to_judge'. Prior screenshots are context, never a canonical baseline.

text.description: Inline design intent: how this UI should look / the invariants that matter.

path.description: Repo-relative path to a design doc / design-system the judge should read.

url.description: Link to the design system / reference (https://…).

routes.description: The bounded list of pages/states to judge — NOT 'the whole UI'. Each is reached by a direct URL.

name.description: A short label used in the screenshot filename + the verdict.

url.description: The direct URL of the page/state (e.g. http://localhost:3000/settings).

viewports.description: Viewports to capture each route at. Defaults to desktop 1440x900; add mobile (390x844) only when you want it judged.
